intent.operator_problem: Pit boss must produce a shift handoff document for incoming supervisor and GM. Today this requires visiting 5-6 screens and manually transcribing numbers — slow, error-prone, and erodes operator trust in a system that records everything but cannot produce a single summary.
intent.pilot_fit: PT-2 sells management-grade operational proof. Without a report, the system captures events but cannot present them as a coherent narrative. The data already exists across built services; the gap is assembly, canonical rendering, and bounded distribution.
success_outcomes: OUT-1: Single page consolidates data from enumerated services into a structured shift report
OUT-2: Financial totals match existing shift dashboard — read-only view, not second source of truth
OUT-3: Report answers three questions: what the floor produced, what was abnormal, how trustworthy the picture is
OUT-4: Canonical PDF generated server-side from fixed template using ShiftReportDTO — no business logic in template layer
OUT-5: PDF is professional, standardized, circulation-ready — suitable for printing, email attachment, and archival
OUT-6: On-screen report page is review surface only, not the authoritative artifact
OUT-7: Manual email distribution of canonical PDF supported for defined recipient classes
OUT-8: CSV export limited to Section 1 per-table financial summary rows only
OUT-9: Report loads within 3 seconds for casino with up to 50 tables
OUT-10: Fixed template with controlled pagination, no dashboard chrome, no interactive UI residue
OUT-11: No new database migrations, RPCs, or security model changes
explicit_exclusions: Staff attribution / 'Who acted?' section — no audit query service exists
Scheduled email delivery or recurring distribution automation
Automatic recipient resolution or routing rules engine
Multi-template support or recipient-specific template variants
Historical trend analysis or comparative reporting
Custom report builder or configurable sections
Custom time windows — casino-configured shift boundaries only
New scoring, thresholding, or prioritization logic
Client-side PDF generation as primary artifact path
Independent business logic in template or rendering layer

adjacent_rejected.why_considered: So What brief Question 4 requires accountability signals
adjacent_rejected.why_rejected_now: No staff-action aggregation service exists. Would expand from assembly task to data-layer invention.

adjacent_rejected.why_considered: Pit bosses would benefit from shift-over-shift comparison
adjacent_rejected.why_rejected_now: Requires time-series query pattern and storage model that does not exist. Analytics-grade work.

adjacent_rejected.why_considered: Gap analysis identified these as missing data sources with ready service layers
adjacent_rejected.why_rejected_now: Each adds report section requiring own assembly, DTO, and rendering. Silent scope expansion.
Report is a snapshot, not a dashboard. Mixing metaphors creates UX confusion.

adjacent_rejected.why_considered: Operators may want arbitrary time range reports
adjacent_rejected.why_rejected_now: Drags feature toward generalized reporting semantics. Casino-configured shift boundaries are the natural unit.

adjacent_rejected.why_considered: GREENFIELD proposed closed sessions summary as Section 2 data
adjacent_rejected.why_rejected_now: Existing RPC returns active visitors only. Deriving closed count requires new query path, violating assembly-only constraint.
Browser-specific variability, weaker formatting control, brittle output. Server-generated PDF is the approved path.

entities.name: ShiftReportDTO
entities.description: Read-only composite DTO assembled from existing service outputs. Contains all data needed to render the canonical Shift Report. Display-ready — no business logic downstream.
fields_hint: report_header (casino_name, property_pit, gaming_day, shift_boundary, generated_at, generated_by, reference_id, confidential_marking)

executive_summary (total_drop, total_fills, total_credits, total_win_loss, hold_pct, total_theo, coverage_pct, active_rated_count, active_unrated_count, anomaly_total, anomaly_unresolved, compliance_trigger_count_mtl, compliance_trigger_count_ctr)
financial_summary (per-table rows: table_label, game_type, drop, fills, credits, win_loss, hold_pct, cash_obs_rollup; casino_totals_row)

rating_coverage (total_rated_sessions, total_unrated_sessions, coverage_pct, per_table_coverage_breakdown, active_rated_visitors, active_unrated_visitors, avg_bet_range, total_theo, theo_discrepancy_summary[open_question])

anomalies (total_alerts, severity_breakdown[critical/warning/info], acknowledged_count, unresolved_count, false_positive_count, alert_quality_ack_rate, alert_quality_fp_rate; staff_attribution_excluded)

entities.name: Shift Report PDF
entities.description: Canonical server-generated PDF document from Shift Report Standard Template v1. The authoritative output artifact for printing, email attachment, and archival.

entities.name: Shift Report Standard Template v1
entities.description: Fixed document template with 9 sections in standardized order. Consumes ShiftReportDTO. No business logic — presentational only.

entities.name: Financial Summary CSV
entities.description: CSV export of Section 1 per-table financial summary rows only. No other report sections are CSV-exportable in MVP.
fields_hint: table_label, game_type, drop, fills, credits, win_loss, hold_pct

capabilities.name: assemble_shift_report
capabilities.description: Fetch data from existing services in parallel and compose a display-ready ShiftReportDTO. All business computation occurs here — downstream layers are presentational only.

capabilities.name: render_report_preview
capabilities.description: Render ShiftReportDTO as on-screen review surface with derivative highlighting of actionable items. Review surface only — not the authoritative artifact.

capabilities.name: generate_canonical_pdf
capabilities.description: Generate canonical Shift Report PDF server-side from ShiftReportDTO via Shift Report Standard Template v1. Fixed layout, controlled pagination, no business logic in template layer.

capabilities.name: export_financial_csv
capabilities.description: Generate CSV file from Section 1 per-table financial summary rows only. Browser download trigger.

capabilities.name: send_report_email
capabilities.description: Manual distribution of canonical PDF as email attachment to bounded recipient classes via project email pathway.

ownership.domain_owner: reporting (new — read-only orchestration over existing bounded contexts)

trigger_events.name: operator_requests_shift_report
preconditions: user is authenticated pit_boss or floor_supervisor
gaming day exists in casino configuration
shift boundary is a configured casino shift (swing/day/grave)

trigger_events.name: operator_requests_pdf_generation
preconditions: report has been assembled and rendered on screen

trigger_events.name: operator_requests_email_send
preconditions: at least one recipient selected from defined classes

trigger_events.name: operator_requests_csv_export
preconditions: Section 1 financial summary data is available

timing_constraints: Report assembly must complete within 3 seconds for up to 50 tables
Report uses casino-configured shift boundaries only — no custom time windows
Gaming day / shift derivation uses existing shift dashboard logic

business_rules.statement: The canonical report artifact is a server-generated PDF from a fixed template. Browser print is a convenience fallback only.
All business computation must occur in WS1 (Report Assembly). The template and PDF generation layers introduce no independent business logic. The document layer is DTO-first and presentational only.
Report highlighting must be purely derivative of existing service states. No new interpretive scoring, thresholding, or prioritization logic.
Financial totals on the report must match the existing shift dashboard. The report is a read-only view of authoritative data, not a second source of truth.
CSV export applies only to Section 1 per-table financial summary rows. No other report sections are exportable in MVP.
Report uses casino-configured shift boundaries only. Custom time windows are excluded from MVP.
No new database migrations, RPCs, views, or security model changes. If implementation cannot proceed with existing data paths, the feature must pause for amendment.
Manual email distribution only. No scheduled delivery, recurring automation, routing rules, or automatic recipient resolution.
One standard template (Shift Report Standard Template v1) in MVP. No multi-template support, recipient-specific variants, or configurable layouts.

The canonical report document must be free of navigation chrome, interactive controls, and screen-responsive layout dependency. Fixed section order, controlled pagination, suitable for print and PDF archival.

invariants: Report is a point-in-time snapshot, not a live dashboard
On-screen page is review surface only — not the authoritative artifact
Canonical generation pipeline: ShiftReportDTO → fixed template → server-generated PDF
Client-side PDF generation is not the primary artifact path
Closed-session count is excluded from MVP (assembly-only constraint)
decision_notes: Frozen architectural direction: canonical DTO → fixed template → server-generated PDF
Browser print downgraded from primary output to convenience fallback (amendment v0.2)
Closed-session count resolved as Option B — removed from MVP (mandate #3)
Server-side PDF generation moved into scope from exclusion list (amendment v0.2)
Client-side PDF explicitly rejected as primary path (amendment v0.3)

loop.system_response: shows date picker (gaming day) and shift selector based on casino-configured shift boundaries only (swing/day/grave)

loop.action: selects gaming day and configured shift boundary
loop.system_response: fetches and assembles data from existing services in parallel, shows loading state, constructs ShiftReportDTO
pit boss sees structured document with financial summary, rating activity, compliance triggers, anomaly/exception status, baseline quality, and loyalty liability — all on one page
highlights sections with actionable items — derivative of existing service states only, no new scoring/thresholding
generates canonical Shift Report PDF server-side from ShiftReportDTO via Shift Report Standard Template v1 with controlled pagination

loop.action: clicks Export CSV on Section 1 financial summary
loop.system_response: generates CSV of per-table financial rows only, triggers browser download
presents bounded recipient selection (incoming supervisor, GM, finance, compliance), sends canonical PDF as email attachment
self-contained PDF document readable without system access

required_existing: GET /api/v1/shift-dashboards/summary — BFF for casino+pits+tables financial metrics (Section 1, 5)
missing_dependencies: Staff audit query service — blocks 'Who acted?' section (FIB-H-A1 amendment)
Closed-session count query — removed from MVP, deferred to amendment
assumptions: All data sources already exist and are API-served
No new migrations or RPCs needed
Gaming day / shift time window uses existing shift dashboard derivation logic
Email service is operational for PDF attachment sending
Existing BFF endpoint meets 3-second SLA for up to 50 tables

Financial totals match existing shift dashboard — read-only view, not second source of truth
Report answers three questions: what the floor produced, what was abnormal, how trustworthy the picture is
Canonical PDF generated server-side from fixed template using ShiftReportDTO
PDF is professional, standardized, circulation-ready for printing, email, and archival
On-screen page is review surface only — not authoritative artifact
Manual email distribution of canonical PDF to defined recipient classes
CSV export limited to Section 1 per-table financial summary rows
Report loads within 3 seconds for up to 50 tables
Fixed template, controlled pagination, no dashboard chrome
No new database migrations, RPCs, or security model changes

Theo discrepancy: include as quality badge in Section 2 or defer?
